statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input is a space-separated list of integers, denoted by n, followed by n space-separated integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the longest increasing subsequence sum.
statement.notes: Time complexity O(n^2) and space complexity O(n).

examples.explanation: The longest increasing subsequence [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] has a sum of 15.

hints.text: Use dynamic programming to calculate the maximum sum of the longest increasing subsequence.

editorial.title: Incorrect Longest Increasing Subsequence Sum
editorial.intuition: The idea is to use dynamic programming to calculate the maximum sum of the longest increasing subsequence.
editorial.optimalSolution: Create a 2D table dp where dp[i][j] represents the maximum sum of the longest increasing subsequence ending at index i with the last element being j. Then, fill up the table using a bottom-up approach.
